title: Self-Introduction Pair Work
titleJa: じこしょうかい ペアワーク
description: Practice introducing yourself and asking about your partner's name, major, and year in school.
canDo: Can introduce yourself and exchange basic personal information with a partner.
sampleDialogue: はじめまして。わたしは＿＿です。＿＿ねんせいです。せんこうは＿＿です。
practicePrompts: Introduce yourself with your name, year, and major.
Ask your partner what year they are and what their major is.
Find something you have in common and respond with も.

title: Responding to Introductions
titleJa: じこしょうかいにこたえる
description: React to what your partner shares in their self-introduction and ask natural follow-up questions.
canDo: Can respond to a partner's introduction, show interest, and ask appropriate follow-up questions.
sampleDialogue: せんこうはけいざいです。そうですか。わたしもけいざいです！
practicePrompts: Listen to your partner's introduction and react with そうですか or わたしも.
Ask a follow-up question about something your partner mentioned, such as their university or country.
Close the conversation politely using よろしくおねがいします and notice what your partner says back.

title: Shopping Pair Work
titleJa: かいもの ペアワーク
description: Practice asking about items and prices in a shop using これ, それ, and あれ.
canDo: Can ask about items in a store and understand prices in Japanese.
sampleDialogue: すみません、これはいくらですか。
practicePrompts: Point to an item and ask how much it costs using これ/それ/あれ.
Ask the clerk what a specific item is using なんですか.
Confirm the price and say you will buy it.

title: At a Department Store
titleJa: デパートでかいもの
description: Point out items you want, ask about available colours and sizes, and respond to a clerk's suggestions.
canDo: Can ask about colour and size options for an item and react to a clerk's suggestions in a shop.
sampleDialogue: それ、あおいろはありますか。はい、ございます。こちらはいかがですか。
practicePrompts: Point to something in the display and ask if it comes in a different colour.
When the clerk shows you an option, react by saying whether you like it or prefer another.
Ask if a smaller or larger size is available and decide whether to buy the item.

title: Daily Routine Pair Work
titleJa: まいにちのせいかつ ペアワーク
description: Practice describing your daily routine using verbs and time expressions with a partner.
canDo: Can describe daily activities and ask about a partner's routine using time expressions.
sampleDialogue: まいにちなんじにおきますか。しちじにおきます。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner what time they wake up, eat, and go to school.
Describe your own daily schedule from morning to evening.
Ask your partner what they do on weekends using しゅうまつ.

title: Morning Routine Interview
titleJa: あさのルーティンインタビュー
description: Interview your partner about their morning routine step by step and note any differences from your own.
canDo: Can ask detailed follow-up questions about a partner's morning schedule and comment on similarities and differences.
sampleDialogue: なんじにあさごはんをたべますか。そうですか、わたしはたべません。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner about each step of their morning: waking up, breakfast, leaving home.
When your partner answers, say whether you do the same thing or something different.
Tell your partner one thing that surprised you about their morning routine.

title: Weekend Plans Pair Work
titleJa: しゅうまつのよてい ペアワーク
description: Practice discussing weekend plans including where you will go and what you will do.
canDo: Can discuss weekend plans with a partner, including locations and activities.
sampleDialogue: しゅうまつ、どこにいきますか。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner where they will go this weekend.
Tell your partner what you plan to do and where.
Invite your partner to do something together using いっしょに.

title: Plans vs. Reality
titleJa: よていとじっさい
description: Discuss what you planned to do last weekend and compare it with what actually happened.
canDo: Can describe a mismatch between plans and what actually happened, responding to a partner's account.
sampleDialogue: えいがをみるつもりでした。でも、いえにいました。そうですか、どうして？
practicePrompts: Tell your partner one thing you planned to do last weekend but did not do.
When your partner shares their plan vs. reality, ask why things changed.
React with expressions like そうですか、たいへんでしたね or いいですね and explain your own weekend.

title: Describing Things Pair Work
titleJa: ものをせつめいする ペアワーク
description: Practice describing objects and people using い-adjectives and な-adjectives.
canDo: Can describe objects and people using basic adjectives in conversation.
sampleDialogue: にほんごのクラスはどうですか。おもしろいですよ。
practicePrompts: Describe your Japanese class using adjectives like おもしろい or むずかしい.
Ask your partner to describe their town or neighborhood.
Describe a person you both know using adjectives.

title: Guessing Game: What Is It?
titleJa: なんですか？ゲーム
description: Describe an object using only adjectives and category clues — without saying its name — while your partner guesses.
canDo: Can use adjectives creatively to describe an object and ask clarifying yes/no questions to guess what it is.
sampleDialogue: おおきくて、しろいです。たべものです。なんですか。アイスクリームですか。
practicePrompts: Think of an everyday object and describe it using at least two adjectives without saying the name.
When your partner is describing, ask yes/no questions like たべものですか or たかいですか.
After your partner guesses, confirm or give one more clue using も or でも.

title: Giving Directions Pair Work
titleJa: みちあんない ペアワーク
description: Practice asking for and giving directions to places around town.
canDo: Can ask for and give simple directions to nearby locations.
sampleDialogue: すみません、ゆうびんきょくはどこですか。
practicePrompts: Ask where a specific place is, such as the post office or bank.
Give directions using みぎ, ひだり, and まっすぐ.
Ask how long it takes to get somewhere using どのぐらい.

title: Lost Tourist Asks for Directions
titleJa: まいごのかんこうきゃく
description: Play a lost tourist who must stop strangers, apologise for the interruption, ask for directions, and confirm you understood.
canDo: Can approach a stranger politely, ask for directions to a landmark, and confirm the route by repeating it back.
sampleDialogue: すみません、えきはどこですか。まっすぐいって、みぎにまがってください。わかりました。ありがとうございます。
practicePrompts: Stop your partner politely using すみません and explain you are lost.
After your partner gives directions, repeat the route back to confirm: まっすぐいって、それからみぎですね。
Ask how far it is on foot and thank your partner before leaving.

title: Weekend Recap Pair Work
titleJa: しゅうまつのはなし ペアワーク
description: Practice talking about what you did last weekend using past tense verbs.
canDo: Can describe past weekend activities and ask about a partner's weekend.
sampleDialogue: しゅうまつ、なにをしましたか。えいがをみました。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner what they did last weekend.
Describe three things you did over the weekend in past tense.
Ask follow-up questions about your partner's activities using どうでしたか.

title: Reporting What You Heard
titleJa: ともだちのはなし
description: Tell your partner what a mutual friend said they did over the weekend, then swap roles and respond to what you hear.
canDo: Can relay in past tense what someone else did and react naturally when hearing secondhand news.
sampleDialogue: ケンさんはしゅうまつにハイキングをしたといっていました。そうですか、いいですね！
practicePrompts: Report one thing a mutual friend did last weekend using past tense and といっていました.
When your partner shares news about the friend, react with そうですか, いいですね, or たいへんでしたね.
Ask a follow-up question about the friend, such as who they went with or how it was.

title: Short Speech: Describing a Person
titleJa: スピーチ: ひとをしょうかいする
description: Prepare and deliver a short speech describing someone you know well.
canDo: Can give a short speech describing a person's appearance, personality, and habits.
sampleDialogue: わたしのともだちをしょうかいします。なまえはリサです。
practicePrompts: Introduce a friend by describing their appearance and personality.
Talk about what the person likes to do and where they are from.
Answer questions from the audience about the person you described.

title: Comparing Two People
titleJa: ふたりをくらべる
description: Compare yourself to a family member, describing similarities and differences in appearance, personality, and habits.
canDo: Can compare two people using contrast expressions and respond to questions about the similarities and differences.
sampleDialogue: わたしはせがたかいですが、あにはひくいです。でも、ふたりともまじめです。
practicePrompts: Describe one way you and a family member are similar using も or ふたりとも.
Describe one clear difference between you using が or でも.
When your partner presents their comparison, ask one follow-up question about the family member.

title: Making Requests Pair Work
titleJa: おねがいする ペアワーク
description: Practice making and responding to requests using てください.
canDo: Can make polite requests and respond to requests from others.
sampleDialogue: すみません、もういちどいってください。
practicePrompts: Ask the teacher to repeat something or speak more slowly.
Request permission to do something using てもいいですか.
Politely ask your partner to help you with something.

title: Declining Requests Politely
titleJa: おねがいをことわる
description: Your partner makes requests; practise declining politely by explaining you cannot or are not supposed to do something.
canDo: Can decline a request politely using てはいけません or できません and offer an alternative where possible.
sampleDialogue: ここでたべてもいいですか。すみません、ここではたべてはいけません。あそこはどうですか。
practicePrompts: When your partner asks if they can do something, decline politely using てはいけません or できません.
After declining, suggest an alternative location or time using あそこ or あとで.
Practise a softer refusal by saying ちょっと… and trailing off, then explain why.

title: Comparing Preferences Pair Work
titleJa: すきなものをくらべる ペアワーク
description: Practice comparing two things and expressing preferences with a partner.
canDo: Can compare two items and express which one you prefer.
sampleDialogue: うみとやま、どちらがすきですか。うみのほうがすきです。
practicePrompts: Ask your partner to choose between two foods and explain why.
Compare two places you have visited using のほうが.
Ask which season your partner likes best using いちばん.

title: Ranking Three Options
titleJa: みっつのなかでいちばん
description: Rank three given options — food, sports, places — and explain your ranking using いちばん and つぎに.
canDo: Can rank three options in order of preference and give a brief reason for each position.
sampleDialogue: みっつのなかで、いちばんすきなのはすしです。つぎにラーメンです。どうしてですか。
practicePrompts: Your partner names three options; rank them from favourite to least favourite using いちばん and つぎに.
Give a short reason for your top choice using から.
Ask your partner how their ranking compares to yours and react to any differences.

title: Expressing Wants and Desires Pair Work
titleJa: ほしいもの・したいこと ペアワーク
description: Practice expressing what you want to do and what you want using たい and ほしい.
canDo: Can express desires and things you want to do, and ask about a partner's wishes.
sampleDialogue: なつやすみに、なにがしたいですか。にほんにいきたいです。
practicePrompts: Tell your partner three things you want to do during vacation using たい.
Ask your partner what they want for their birthday using ほしい.
Discuss a place you both want to visit and why.

title: Dream Trip Planning
titleJa: ゆめのりょこうをけいかくする
description: Discuss a dream holiday together — where you want to go, what you want to see and eat, and what you want to buy.
canDo: Can discuss a dream trip using たい and ほしい, respond to a partner's ideas, and build on their suggestions.
sampleDialogue: イタリアにいきたいです。なにがしたいですか。パスタをたべたいし、ゆうめいなびじゅつかんにいきたいです。
practicePrompts: Tell your partner your dream destination and at least two things you want to do there using たい.
React to your partner's ideas with expressions like いいですね or わたしもいきたいです.
Together, agree on one activity you both want to do and explain why using から.

title: Giving Advice Pair Work
titleJa: アドバイスする ペアワーク
description: Practice giving and receiving advice using たほうがいい and ないほうがいい.
canDo: Can give simple advice and suggest what someone should or should not do.
sampleDialogue: にほんごがじょうずになりたいです。まいにちれんしゅうしたほうがいいですよ。
practicePrompts: Your partner has a problem; give advice using たほうがいい.
Suggest something your partner should avoid using ないほうがいい.
Ask for advice about improving your Japanese study habits.

title: Getting Advice About a Problem
titleJa: もんだいをそうだんする
description: Describe a problem you have to your partner and receive their advice, then respond to show whether you will follow it.
canDo: Can explain a personal problem clearly enough for a partner to give advice, and respond to that advice with agreement or a counter-reason.
sampleDialogue: さいきん、ねむれないんです。そうですか。はやくねたほうがいいですよ。そうですね、やってみます。
practicePrompts: Describe a simple problem you have — tiredness, not understanding class, etc. — to your partner.
When your partner gives advice using たほうがいい or ないほうがいい, respond with そうですね and say whether you will try it.
If the advice is hard to follow, explain why using でも or ちょっとむずかしいです and ask for another suggestion.
